# @json-express/transport-express — AI Context

The default HTTP transport (part of `@json-express/boot`), built on Express 5. You never see Express — routes come from the API generator; this layer only speaks HTTP.

## Install — that is the whole integration
```
npm i @json-express/transport-express
```
Discovery is automatic. **Never import express, add express middleware, or touch `app` — per-request logic belongs in `middleware-*` packages; endpoints belong in `models/`.**

## Configuration (.env — case-insensitive)
| Key | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `jex.port` | `3000` | Listen port (global key, read by the runner) |
| `jex.express.ssl` | (none) | TLS key/cert pair — normally injected by `plugin-devcert`, not set by hand |
| `jex.security.headers` | `true` | Baseline response headers (nosniff, frameguard, referrer-policy; HSTS on https). Set `false` to opt out. |
| `jex.errors.verbose` | (mode) | Override 5xx masking. Defaults to masked when `jex.mode=production`, verbose otherwise. |

## Behavior contract
- Consistent JSON errors: unknown route → `404 { statusCode, error }`; handler failure → JSON 500.
- **Production masks 5xx**: in `jex.mode=production` a 500 returns `{ error: "Internal Server Error", traceId }`; the full detail is logged server-side. Dev shows the real message. Override with `jex.errors.verbose`.
- Every response carries baseline security headers unless `jex.security.headers=false`.
- Fails loudly on EADDRINUSE (never silently picks another port).
- Per-request `traceId` correlation and access logs out of the box.
